sourceRef.citation: Rayyan Ramadhan Ramadhan Erlangga Putra et al. 2026

statement.en: Adaptive Waste Collection and Distribution Vessel for the Ciliwung and Pesanggrahan Rivers in Jakarta
statement.summary: Jakarta’s rivers have become highly polluted with waste, especially plastics, exacerbating urban flood risk. Conventional cleanup measures – such as floating trash booms, sediment dredging, and powered cleaning boats – often fail under strong currents or sudden water level rises. In this study, we use a qualitative case-study and design-research framework (observation, data collection, analysis, design recommendation) to develop an innovative, adaptive river-cleaning vessel. The proposed design adapts dynamically to changing flow and water height without obstructing the river, while emphasizing aesthetic form, sustainable materials, and environmental education. Initial results suggest this concept could significantly improve upon existing trash boats and serve as a practical solution for g

proposer: Rayyan Ramadhan Ramadhan Erlangga Putra, Ariani, Krishna Hutama et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom describes a design-research proposal (qualitative case study) for a vessel prototype addressing real Jakarta river pollution. Status is explicitly provisional: abstract is truncated ('serve as a practical solution for g...'), no peer review date given, and the work appears to be design recommendations rather than validated empirical results or proven engineering performance. The concept is plausible and motivated, but unvalidated—characteristic of preprint/working paper status.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 川の流量と水位の変化に合わせて、容器自体が呼吸するように形を変える。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 硬い構造ではなく、流れの中で柔らかく呼応し、塵芥を優しく捕える有機的な形態。
